memory_recovered.statement: The operational queue is an append-only history: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json is a bootstrap snapshot, while immutable operations/queue-updates records must be discovered completely and replayed deterministically to derive effective current state.
memory_recovered.evidence_strength: strong for repository architecture; incomplete for repository-wide discovery coverage

observed: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json contains M-001 as the latest continuity item in its embedded snapshot.
A later immutable commit records completed continuity item M-006 and hands off M-007.
The discovery-manifest adapter already distinguishes verified record validity from discovery completeness.
inferred: Reading ACTIVE_QUEUE.json alone can produce a stale continuity state.
Reading additive records without a deterministic replay rule can produce conflicting or order-dependent state.
proposed: Treat complete discovery plus deterministic replay over the base snapshot as the authoritative queue read path.
Reject cross-owner updates and duplicate record identifiers rather than overwriting prior team state.
superseded: Treating the most recently fetched queue representation as authoritative regardless of provenance.
Overwriting canonical queue history to make the snapshot appear current.
unresolved: The available connector did not expose an exhaustive paginated directory or tree traversal in this run.
The committed Node tests could not be executed because the runtime had no network path to clone the repository.

behavior: fails closed when discovery completeness is unproven
replays updates by timestamp then record_id

verification.runtime_blocker: container could not resolve github.com, so a fresh checkout was unavailable

privacy_boundary: Only public repository metadata, abstract operational claims, and non-personal queue identifiers were persisted. No private chat, health, credentials, identifying personal data, or unreviewed uploads were included.
falsification_route: Provide a complete repository inventory showing no later queue-update records than the base snapshot.
Demonstrate that deterministic replay produces a different result from the immutable record sequence.
Show that an accepted update mutates another team's ownership or that duplicate record IDs are silently applied.
